svg.title: Customer access can substitute for ownership—and customer conflict polarizes it
svg.desc: Two phase maps compare a homogeneous six-asset network with a heterogeneous network of clinics, laboratories, and payers. The horizontal axis is the fraction of learning available through arm's-length customer access. The vertical axis is the share of cross-boundary customer value placed at risk by partial ownership. At high customer conflict, each map separates into a neutral platform and a full rollup. At low conflict, partial rollups appear.
